feature-two-column-scene.props: {"headline":"Jira Sync","subhead":"Pull Jira issues directly into your repository","bodyPrimary":"Bring external planning context into local files so agents can execute without API dependencies.","leftRatio":0.4,"rightPanel":"pictogram","pictogramType":"jira","pictogramScale":1.22}

{"headline":"Jira Sync","subhead":"Pull Jira issues directly into your repository","bodyPrimary":"Run one command to sync the project and give every coding agent full issue context locally.","bodySecondary":"Sync operations are idempotent and safe to rerun, preserving stable local Kanbus IDs without duplication.","leftRatio":0.4,"rightPanel":"jira-sync-demo"}

cue.label: Introduction
cue.voice: Pull Jira issues into your repository so coding agents always have full context. No API calls, no MCP tools, just files.

cue.label: Sync
cue.voice: Run a single command to sync your entire project. Kanbus fetches every issue and translates types, statuses, and priorities automatically.

cue.label: Idempotent
cue.voice: Safe to re-run anytime. Existing issues are updated in place, keeping the local Kanbus IDs stable across syncs.

cue.label: Agents
cue.voice: Any agent that can read your source code can now read your Jira issues. The context travels with your branch and survives offline.
